title: phyllanthus manausensis
intro: Phyllanthus manausensis, also known as Manaus tree or Piquiá, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine in the Amazon region, particularly in Brazil, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Phyllanthus manausensis include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and burns. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used in teas, infusions, or extracts, which are said to have a range of therapeutic effects when consumed.
